prompt: Set up a HarborWorks Booking & Group Reservation Hub that enables members and staff to reserve limited-capacity sessions (e.g., equipment bays, guided classes, or inspection appointments) and optionally form group-buys that unlock discounted pricing once a headcount threshold is met. Regular Users register and sign in on an English web interface rendered with Templ, where they can search availability by date and time, see remaining capacity in real time, and place a booking that immediately shows a clear state label such as Pending Confirmation, Waitlisted, Checked In, Completed, or Canceled. Users can start or join a group-buy tied to a specific session; the UI must display the deadline (e.g., “forms by 9:00 PM”) and progress (e.g., “3 of 5 members joined”), and provide immediate feedback on duplicate clicks with a single, stable order outcome. Users can download or view booking documents generated from templates, such as a confirmation PDF and a check-in pass image, with visible version history and a “superseded” badge when a newer revision is issued. An in-app Notification & To-Do Center shows actionable items like “Approve exception,” “Check in next appointment,” or “Group formed—confirm,” with filters by status and a delivery log view for Admins. An Operations dashboard provides offline analytics for views, favorites, comments, and downloads, showing top sessions and 7/30/90-day trends plus an anomaly banner when traffic spikes above 3× the trailing 7-day hourly average. The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Templ-rendered UI, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for users, roles, bookings, group-buys, documents, notifications, and event analytics. Authentication is strictly local username/password; passwords must be at least 12 characters with a strength check (upper/lower/number/symbol), stored as salted hashes, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, login is locked for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, and CAPTCHA is required starting on the 3rd failed attempt. The booking workflow is enforced by a state machine with server-side validation: lead time minimum 2 hours, cutoff 10 minutes before start, max 3 active bookings per user per day, blacklist blocks all new bookings, and duplicate-booking validation prevents overlapping time windows. Group-buy slot locking must prevent oversell using optimistic locking on remaining capacity plus an idempotency key per client action; a group succeeds when it reaches a configurable threshold (default 5) before a deadline (default 24 hours), otherwise it transitions to Failed and releases pre-allocated slots. Data governance includes a unified local data dictionary and tagging framework, CSV import/export with bulk validation (rejecting invalid dates, capacities, and duplicates), caching with explicit TTL (60 seconds) and cache-bypass for Admin review screens, and retry-safe handlers with at-most-once effects via idempotency records. Privacy is enforced with consent tracking, field-level masking for names on shared views, encryption at rest for sensitive notes using a locally managed key, and a self-service deletion flow that hard-deletes personal data within 7 days while retaining anonymized analytics. Backups are stored on local removable media with daily incrementals and weekly full backups, and a documented restore procedure must recover to the last full backup plus incrementals within 4 hours in a single-site outage. Integration is offline-only via file-based exchange and locally hosted webhooks within the same network, supporting configurable field mapping and callback retries with exponential backoff capped at 5 attempts.
